ops.text: A Workflow codex-wrapper agent (impl:home-origin-floor, run wf_4ed408de-37d) fully implemented its work order — shortlist.py +33 with passing tests +57 — then died on "StructuredOutput retry cap (5) exceeded", so pipeline() dropped the lane to null and the workflow reported the implementation as a failure while the edits sat silently in the working tree. Inverse of the earlier no-op-reported-as-done papercuts: real work reported as no-work. The orchestrator only caught it by diffing the tree after the run. Mitigation: after any workflow with implementation lanes, diff the tree against the reported files_changed union before dispatching redo work — a "failed" lane may have finished everything but the paperwork; and schema-forced wrapper agents apparently can exhaust StructuredOutput retries after a long codex session, so keep wrapper schemas small or accept prose returns for implementation lanes.
